narrative:
Yoane Wissa is a Centre-Forward playing for France. His overall rating of 7.12/10 places him in the top 6% of centre-forwards at this tournament. This rating reflects 80% from club form over the last two seasons and 20% from this World Cup.

At this World Cup, Wissa has demonstrated clinical finishing, scoring 0.75 goals per 90 minutes from an expected goals rate of 0.622 per 90 minutes. He averages 2.5 shots per 90 minutes, indicating his consistent threat in front of goal. However, his expected assists of 0.015 per 90 minutes and 0.25 key passes per 90 minutes suggest limited creative output for teammates.

Wissa functions as a crucial attacking focal point for France, primarily tasked with finding the back of the net. Scouts and fans should watch for his continued ability to convert chances, especially given his strong World Cup goals per 90 minutes compared to his club level. His impact will largely depend on his direct contributions in the final third during remaining matches.
